program.name: Telstra Graduate Program
program.salary_text: When you start in the Program, you’ll be given a competitive salary.
program.duration_text: The Graduate Program runs for 14 months, which includes a month-long induction at the commencement.
program.rotation_text: During this 14-month period you will be allocated into Chapters/Operative groups and assigned to missions/projects to develop capabilities in chosen pathways.

eligibility.work_rights: You must be eligible to work in Australia. International students who have a valid visa that enables them to work in Australia without any work restrictions are eligible to apply to technical pathways only.
eligibility.graduation_window: For the 2026 intake, you must have completed your most recent tertiary education qualification within the last three years up to the Program start in February 2026 (i.e. graduating between 2023, 2024 and 2025).
minimum_requirements: You must have completed a minimum of an undergraduate degree at an Australian University within the last three years before the commencement of the Program in February 2026 (i.e. graduating between 2023, 2024 and 2025).

stages.name: Submit application
stages.details: Fill out a short online application form to check your eligibility. Upload your resume and most recent academic transcript.

stages.name: Video interview and online assessment (HireVue)
stages.details: Complete a video interview and an online assessment using HireVue.

stages.name: Final interview (virtual)
stages.details: This includes a graduate ambassador Q&A session, a case study where you prepare a 3–5 minute pitch, and a behavioural interview with motivational and behavioural questions.

stages.name: Checks
stages.details: Telstra may run checks such as security or background checks before moving to the final stage.

stages.name: Offer
stages.details: If successful, Telstra will call you with an offer. If you miss out, they may keep you in their talent pool and contact you later if roles become available.

tips: Before you start the HireVue stage, make sure you have a phone, PC or laptop, and a working microphone and camera.
Do the interview and assessments in a quiet place with minimal distractions.
Check your internet connection before you start.
Use the chance to practice before recording your HireVue answers.
If you make a mistake in HireVue, you can re-record (up to three attempts per question).
It is okay to use notes during HireVue, and it is fine to briefly break eye contact to check them.
Read about Telstra’s T25 strategy, purpose, and four core values, and re-read the role description for your chosen pathway.

exec_themes: Connected Future 30 strategy: “double down” on connectivity and digital infrastructure, and “radically innovate” in the core network (including treating the network as a product)
Cost discipline and efficiency focus across the business (including targeting positive operating leverage, and keeping tight control of costs and BAU capex)
Customer experience and brand outcomes as strategic goals (e.g., lifting strategic NPS and being a top-10 brand)
Mobile and network leadership investment (e.g., building reliability/resilience and advancing 5G capabilities)
Reset/turnaround actions in the Enterprise business (noted as under-performing, with actions underway)
Sustainability positioned as a strategy enabler (“Operating Sustainably”), with emissions and renewable energy commitments tied to long-term value
commercial_context.profit_engine: Telstra’s profit engine is built around earning cashflows from its core connectivity business (especially mobiles) and monetising its digital infrastructure assets, while protecting margins through cost reduction/cost discipline and disciplined capital management (dividends, potential buy-backs, and portfolio actions).
commercial_context.headwinds: Telstra highlights that its Enterprise business has under-performed and that resetting it “will take time.” It also describes operating in a fast-changing, highly competitive environment with changing customer needs and ongoing pressure to keep investing in networks and digital infrastructure; it notes prior NBN-related “economic headwind” as context for its turnaround period.
commercial_context.esg: Telstra frames sustainability around two main pillars—Environment and Communities—supported by a commitment to “doing business responsibly.” Disclosed priorities and targets include reducing absolute Scope 1+2 emissions by 70% and Scope 3 emissions by 50% by 2030 (from an FY2019 baseline), progressing renewable energy generation equivalent to Telstra’s electricity consumption (targeting 100% by end of 2025), and community-focused programs aimed at digital inclusion and supporting customers in vulnerable circumstances.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: In May 2025, Telstra signalled a strategic shift from the T22/T25 strategy cycle to a new five-year plan called “Connected Future 30,” including a stronger emphasis on treating the network as a product (commercial value capture via network attributes and APIs), stepping up cost discipline/efficiency, and continuing to leverage mobile and digital infrastructure leadership.

notes: Program duration (14 months) and the induction timing are stated on Telstra’s Graduate Program page.
Pathways (streams) are listed under the 'Choose your pathway' section.
Specific program locations are not stated on the pages used.
Work rights and the graduation window for the February 2026 start are stated in the Program eligibility section and FAQs.
Eligible disciplines are not explicitly listed as degree fields.
The Student & Grad application stages are described on Telstra’s 'How we hire' page.
Interview preparation tips for Students & Grads (HireVue) are listed on the same page.

Primary strategy and financial-discipline themes are directly supported by Telstra’s 27 May 2025 ASX market release on the Connected Future 30 strategy and the 27 May 2025 Investor Day transcript (Connected Future 30).

ESG themes and some metrics (e.g., emissions reduction progress, renewable energy coverage) are supported by Telstra’s Sustainability Hub pages; however, the 2025 Bigger Picture Sustainability Report PDF could not be fetched by the tool at time of extraction (tool returned an error), so ESG detail is constrained to what is explicitly visible on Telstra web pages and the Connected Future 30 market release.

Headwinds are summarized from explicit statements about Enterprise under-performance and the need for an Enterprise “reset,” plus broad competitive/technology change context; more granular headwinds (e.g., specific regulatory, pricing, or macro pressures) were not extracted because they were not clearly spelled out in the retrieved official sources.
